title: Alnylam — Financials
html:
Module 3: Financial Architecture & Budget Intelligence

Governing Thought

Alnylam's financial profile is defined by explosive growth in the TTR franchise ($2.5B in 2025, guided to $4.4-4.7B in 2026 -- 83% YoY growth) alongside a mature, steady rare disease portfolio (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO ~$500M combined). This growth trajectory makes Alnylam's market research budget a moving target -- as revenue nearly doubles, research investment should expand proportionally. The financial intelligence below triangulates Alnylam's public financials, ZoomRx's project portfolio, and inferred budget dynamics. Significant gaps remain in ZoomRx-side financial data (revenue, pricing, cluster allocation) that should be populated from Kimble and the AO plan.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble ZoomRx revenue per project, effort tracking, invoicing Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
Alnylam Earnings Reports Company-level revenue, guidance, strategic priorities Public (SEC filings, earnings calls) Quarterly

Alnylam Company Financial Profile

Revenue Trajectory

Year TTR Franchise GIVLAARI + OXLUMO Total Net Product Revenue YoY Growth
2024 ~$2,100M (est.) ~$424M ~$2,524M --
2025 $2,487M (AMVUTTRA $2,314M + ONPATTRO $173M) ~$500M (combined) ~$2,987M ~18%
2026 Guidance $4,400-$4,700M Mid-single-digit growth ~$5,300M total ~77%
2030 Target Dominant driver Steady $10B+ "Alnylam 2030"

Key financial dynamics:

  • AMVUTTRA is the growth engine: $2,314M in 2025, on track for $4,400-4,700M in 2026. The ATTR-CM approval (March 2025) is the inflection point
  • ONPATTRO is declining ($173M) as patients transition to AMVUTTRA; still relevant in France
  • GIVLAARI + OXLUMO combined ~$500M; mid-single-digit growth guided for 2026. These are mature rare disease products
  • Q4 2025 rare disease quarterly revenue: $137M combined (26% YoY growth)
  • GIVLAARI patient growth: ~15% YoY (driven by new patient starts from diagnosis expansion)
  • OXLUMO patient growth: ~20% YoY

Revenue by Brand

Brand FY2025 Revenue Growth Driver Revenue Trajectory
AMVUTTRA $2,314M ATTR-CM launch ramp; cardiologist penetration; 12,000+ patients Explosive growth (83% guided)
ONPATTRO $173M Legacy PN patients; France market Declining (transitioning to AMVUTTRA)
GIVLAARI ~$300M+ (est.) New AHP diagnoses; chronic symptom expansion Steady (mid-single-digit growth)
OXLUMO ~$200M+ (est.) New PH1 diagnoses; all-ages/all-stages positioning Steady (mid-single-digit growth)

Competitive Revenue Context

Understanding Alnylam's competitors' financial scale is critical for interpreting market dynamics:

Competitor Product Revenue Owner Implication
VYNDAQEL/VYNDAMAX (tafamidis) $5.45B (2024) Pfizer Incumbent leader in ATTR-CM; still 2x AMVUTTRA revenue but declining share
ATTRUBY (acoramidis) $362M (first full year) BridgeBio Rapid uptake; 7,800+ prescriptions by Feb 2026
Rivfloza (nedosiran) Not separately disclosed Novo Nordisk Competing with OXLUMO in PH1

ZoomRx Revenue from Alnylam

What We Know

[Populate the following from Kimble and AO plan]

Data Point Value Source Status
Total trailing-12-month ZoomRx revenue from Alnylam [Populate from Kimble] Kimble Needed
AO/AD assignment [Populate] AO plan Needed
Cluster assignment [Populate -- likely East 7 or East 8] Cluster management Needed
Alnylam share of cluster revenue [Populate] Base 1 Needed
Preferred vendor pricing / discount structure [Populate] AO plan / procurement Needed
FY26 target for Alnylam [Populate] AO plan Needed
YTD bookings [Populate] Kimble Needed
Pipeline / proposals pending [Populate] Kimble Needed

Active Workstream Revenue Map (Inferred)

Based on the active project portfolio, the ZoomRx revenue from Alnylam is distributed across these workstreams:

Workstream Revenue Type Cadence Estimated Annual Value
AMVUTTRA US PET (CM) Recurring Quarterly (90 interactions/product/quarter) [Populate from Kimble]
AMVUTTRA US PET (PN) Recurring Semi-annual (60 interactions/wave) [Populate]
AMVUTTRA ATU (ATTR-CM) Recurring Quarterly (90 cardiologists/wave) [Populate]
AMVUTTRA ATU (hATTR-PN) Periodic One wave (80 physicians) [Populate]
AMVUTTRA PCA Recurring Semi-annual (300 CM+Mixed, 100 PN charts) [Populate]
International PET (5 countries) Recurring Semi-annual (2 waves completed) [Populate -- likely significant given multi-country scope]
Patient Message Recall Ad hoc Per request [Populate]
AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy Ad hoc Per request [Populate]
GIVLAARI US PET Recurring Semi-annual [Populate]
GIVLAARI Global ATU (US/Germany/Brazil) Recurring Annual (Q3/Q4 fielding) [Populate]
OXLUMO PET Recurring Semi-annual (head-to-head vs Rivfloza) [Populate]
OXLUMO ATU New Wave 1 (Dec 2024) [Populate]
Integrated Analysis Recurring Quarterly (PET+ATU+PCA integration) [Populate]

Revenue concentration assessment: The AMVUTTRA program (US PET + ATU + PCA + International PET + Integrated Analysis + Strategy) likely accounts for 60-70% of total Alnylam revenue to ZoomRx. Any loss of the AMVUTTRA tracking program would be material. The rare disease studies (GIVLAARI + OXLUMO) provide diversification but at lower total value.

Budget Intelligence (Inferred)

Alnylam's Market Research Investment Trajectory

While we do not have direct visibility into Alnylam's total MR budget, the following can be inferred:

Growth-driven budget expansion: Alnylam's revenue is growing 77% YoY (2025 to 2026). Companies in this growth phase typically increase market research investment proportionally as they:

  • Launch new indications (ATTR-CM launched March 2025)
  • Enter new specialties (cardiology -- entirely new for Alnylam)
  • Expand geographically (international PET already covers 5 countries)
  • Face new competition (ATTRUBY launched Nov 2024, Wainua ATTR-CM data expected mid-2026)

Alnylam-specific signals:

  • The company added ATTRUBY tracking rapidly after its November 2024 approval (90 interactions/quarter in US PET)
  • International PET expanded to 5 countries across 2 waves
  • Integrated analysis (PET+ATU+PCA) was commissioned -- this represents higher-value strategic work
  • AMVUTTRA 2025 Strategy study suggests willingness to invest in ad hoc strategic research
  • Weekly check-in meetings (22+) imply significant ongoing investment in the ZoomRx relationship

Budget risk factors:

  • Alnylam's "Alnylam 2030" strategy launched January 2026 -- this typically triggers investment reviews
  • The new target list (April 2026) reduced available respondents by 56-72%, which may pressure study scope or pricing
  • GIVLAARI and OXLUMO are mature rare disease brands with mid-single-digit growth -- research budgets here may plateau
  • ATTR-CM is the growth investment; expect budget gravity to shift toward TTR franchise research

Pricing Context

Alnylam Drug Pricing

Understanding Alnylam's drug pricing is important context for interpreting access/affordability barriers in the ZoomRx data:

Product Annual Treatment Cost (US) Patient Support
AMVUTTRA ~$300K-400K/year (est.) 99% US commercial coverage; 70% of patients pay $0 OOP
GIVLAARI ~$575K/year at list; ~$442K after discounts Prevalence-based value agreements with payers
OXLUMO ~$493K/year (WAC, weight-based) Alnylam Assist: 94% patients at $0 OOP; 95% payer coverage

Implication for ZoomRx: All three products are priced in the $300K-575K/year range. Access/affordability barriers consistently surface as the #1 or #2 finding in ATU and PET data across AHP and PH1. This is not a messaging problem -- it reflects genuine structural friction in the rare disease specialty pharmacy ecosystem. ZoomRx's value includes tracking how effectively Alnylam's access programs are mitigating these barriers.

Financial Questions to Answer

Pull these from Kimble and AO plan:

Revenue & Pipeline

  • What is Alnylam's total trailing-12-month revenue to ZoomRx?
  • What are the top 5 Alnylam projects by revenue? Which brands do they belong to?
  • What is the current Alnylam pipeline (proposals submitted but not yet won)?
  • What Alnylam SOWs are expiring in the next 90 days and need renewal?
  • What is the International PET revenue specifically? (Multi-country studies are often high-value)

Profitability

  • What is the blended margin on Alnylam work? Is there preferred vendor pricing?
  • Which Alnylam projects are running over-effort?
  • Is the combined OXL+GIV PET readout efficient or does the dual-brand structure create scope creep?

Growth Potential

  • Is there a path to expanding the AMVUTTRA program? (New studies, new countries, deeper integration)
  • Can OXLUMO PET expand to more waves or larger sample sizes?
  • Is there appetite for new study types at Alnylam? (e.g., Patient experience, MSL effectiveness, Digital tracking for ATTR-CM)
  • What would a GIVLAARI PET refresh cost? (Last PET data is Q2 2023 -- 3+ years old)

Watch Items

ATTR-CM Growth = Research Budget Growth (HIGH)

AMVUTTRA's ATTR-CM approval is driving explosive revenue growth. This should translate to expanded market research investment, particularly in cardiology-specific tracking, competitive intelligence against ATTRUBY and Wainua, and international market expansion. ZoomRx should be proactively proposing new study scopes to capture this budget growth before competitors do.

Action: Review AO plan for FY26 Alnylam targets. Identify whether ZoomRx revenue from Alnylam is keeping pace with the company's growth trajectory.

International PET Expansion Opportunity (HIGH)

The 5-country international PET is a high-value engagement that could expand as ATTRUBY/BEYONTTRA launches internationally (Bayer in EU, Alexion in Japan) in 2026-2027. This will make the international competitive landscape more complex, justifying additional countries, more frequent waves, or expanded sample sizes.

Action: Track ATTRUBY international approval timelines as triggers for international PET expansion proposals.

Rare Disease Budget Plateau Risk (MEDIUM)

GIVLAARI and OXLUMO are in mid-single-digit growth mode. Alnylam's strategic attention and budget will increasingly skew toward the TTR franchise. If ZoomRx does not demonstrate strategic value in rare disease tracking (beyond routine PET/ATU), these study budgets may be flat or compressed.

Action: Propose higher-value deliverables in rare disease: diagnostic journey study for AHP, urologist-specific PH1 module, integrated PET+ATU design for GIVLAARI.

New Target List Impact (MEDIUM)

The April 2026 target list change reduced available respondents by 56-72%. This has direct financial implications: smaller recruitment pools may require longer fielding periods, higher incentives, or reduced sample sizes -- all affecting project economics.

Action: Monitor fielding efficiency and cost per complete post-TL change. Flag to Alnylam if recruitment challenges affect data quality.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home

Action: Propose higher-value deliverables in rare disease: diagnostic journey study for AHP, urologist-specific PH1 module, integrated PET+ATU design for GIVLAARI.
